2603.17052.title: Early Quantization Shrinks Codebook: A Simple Fix for Diversity-Preserving Tokenization
2603.17052.paper_summary: Vector quantization is a technique in machine learning that discretizes continuous representations into a set of discrete vectors. It is widely employed in tokenizing data representations for large language models, diffusion models, and other generative models. Despite its prevalence, the characteristics and behaviors of vector quantization in generative models remain largely underexplored. In this study, we systematically investigate the issue of collapses in vector quantization, where collapsed representations are observed across discrete codebook tokens and continuous latent embeddings. By leveraging both synthetic and real datasets, we identify the severity of each type of collapses and triggering conditions. Our analysis reveals that random initialization and limited encoder capacity result in tokens collapse and embeddings collapse. Building on these findings, we propose potential solutions aimed at mitigating each collapse. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first comprehensive study examining representation collapsing problems in vector quantization.
2603.17052.paper_summary_zh: 向量量化是一種機器學習技術，將連續表示離散化為一組離散向量。<lb/>它廣泛應用於大型語言模型、擴散模型和其他生成模型中的數據表示標記化。<lb/>儘管其普遍存在，向量量化在生成模型中的特徵和行為仍然在很大程度上未被探索。<lb/>在這項研究中，我們系統地調查了向量量化中的崩潰問題，其中在離散碼本標記和連續潛在嵌入中觀察到崩潰的表示。<lb/>通過利用合成數據集和真實數據集，我們識別了每種類型崩潰的嚴重性和觸發條件。<lb/>我們的分析顯示，隨機初始化和有限的編碼器容量導致了標記崩潰和嵌入崩潰。<lb/>基於這些發現，我們提出了旨在減輕每種崩潰的潛在解決方案。<lb/>據我們所知，這是第一個全面研究向量量化中表示崩潰問題的研究。
2603.17052.author: Wenhao Zhao et.al.
2603.17052.authors: Wenhao Zhao, Qiran Zou, Rushi Shah, Yudi Wu, Zhouhan Lin, Dianbo Liu
